meta.title: BiBoTracking — Free, Open-Source Hubstaff Alternative
meta.description: A free, open-source Hubstaff alternative for macOS & Windows — track app time, keystrokes, screenshots & browser activity. Local-first, self-hostable, free for 2 years.
meta.ogTitle: BiBoTracking — Free, Open-Source Hubstaff Alternative for macOS & Windows
meta.ogDescription: A free, open-source Hubstaff alternative for macOS & Windows. App & window time, keystrokes, screenshots and browser activity — local-first and self-hostable. Free for 2 years, then $1/month.
meta.ogImageAlt: BiBoTracking — time & activity tracking for macOS & Windows
meta.twitterDescription: Free, open-source, self-hostable employee time & activity tracking for macOS & Windows. Free for 2 years, then $1/month.

jsonld.appDescription: A free, open-source Hubstaff alternative for macOS & Windows. Tracks app & window time, keystroke counts, periodic screenshots and browser activity — local-first and self-hostable.
jsonld.offerPaid: $1/month after 2 years, or self-host for free

q1.name: Is there a free, open-source Hubstaff alternative?
q1.answer: Yes. BiBoTracking is a free, open-source alternative to Hubstaff for macOS & Windows. It tracks app and window time, keystroke counts, screenshots and browser activity, and is free for your first two years (then $1/month) — or self-host it for free, forever.

q2.name: How much does BiBoTracking cost?
q2.answer: It is free for the first two years with every feature included. After that it is a flat $1 per month for the hosted service, with no per-seat pricing. You can also self-host the open-source version for free.

q3.name: Does BiBoTracking record what employees type?
q3.answer: No. The keyboard signal is a count of keypresses only — the actual keys are never read or stored. All activity is stored locally first, and sync to the dashboard is optional and one-way.

q4.name: Can I self-host BiBoTracking?
q4.answer: Yes. The Go backend, web dashboard, desktop app (macOS & Windows) and Chrome extension are open source. One Go binary serves the API and dashboard, backed by Postgres, so you can run the whole stack on your own machine for free.

hero.eyebrow: The open-source Hubstaff alternative · macOS & Windows
hero.ledeB:
for macOS & Windows. BiBoTracking measures
          active app & window time, keystroke volume, periodic screenshots and browser activity —
          stored locally on each computer, and rolled up for owners in a clean web dashboard.
hero.note: Free for 2 years, then $1/month · open source & self-hostable · Universal macOS (Apple Silicon + Intel) & Windows 10+.
hero.dashboardAlt: BiBoTracking desktop dashboard showing active time, top app, keypresses and screenshots

features.title: Everything that matters. Nothing that doesn't.
features.intro: Four lightweight signals give an honest picture of the workday — without screen-scraping content or logging what people type.

time.p: Idle-aware active time per app and window. Idle gaps are hatched out, not counted.

keyboard.p: Counts only — a measure of typing activity. Actual keys are never captured or stored.

screenshots.p: Automatic captures on a set interval, compressed under 50 KB, with an auto-delete retention window.

browser.p: A featherweight Manifest V3 extension reports active page and time — to the local app only.

private.p: Everything lands in a single local SQLite file. Sync to the owner dashboard is optional and one-way.

menubar.p: A quiet menu-bar (system-tray on Windows) indicator shows tracking, idle or paused — and lets you start, stop or open the app.

demo.title: A quick look, both sides.
demo.intro: The desktop app the employee runs, and the web dashboard owners sign in to. Real product, real data.
demo.capDesktop: The desktop app — dashboard, activity, screenshots, browser and org-managed settings.
demo.capWeb: The owner dashboard — drill into any employee's activity, keystrokes, browser and screenshots.

how.title: Up and running in three steps.

step1.p: The employee installs BiBoTracking on macOS or Windows and grants the requested capture permissions (Accessibility, Input Monitoring and Screen Recording on macOS). Status auto-updates the moment they're granted.

step2.pA: Optional. The Manifest V3 extension auto-discovers the local app and reports browser pages to
step2.link: Get it on the Chrome Web Store →

step3.p: Activity syncs one-way to the dashboard, where owners review time, keystrokes, browser history and screenshots per employee.

showcase.title: Built for clarity, on both ends.
showcase.intro: The same data, presented for whoever's looking — flat, minimal, dark or light.

d1.sub: — keystroke volume over the day, counts only.

d3.sub: — top sites by time and page visits.

d4.sub: — capture policy managed by the organization.

w1.sub: — last seen & active time per employee.

w2.sub: — per-app breakdown for any day or range.

compare.title: The same core tracking, without the per-seat bill.
compare.intro: If you use Hubstaff for app, screenshot and activity monitoring, you already know the workflow. This is the open-source, local-first version — for a flat price, or free if you host it yourself.
compare.note: Comparison based on publicly listed Hubstaff plans and features. “Hubstaff” is a trademark of its respective owner; BiBoTracking is not affiliated with or endorsed by Hubstaff.

privacy.title: Monitoring that respects the person.
privacy.intro: Transparency isn't a setting — it's how the product is built.

keys.p: The keyboard signal is a count of presses per bucket. The keys themselves are never read or stored.

local.p: All activity lands in a local SQLite file. Sync to the dashboard is optional, one-way and idempotent.

domain.p: Browser tracking can store just the domain instead of the full URL — your policy, your call.

retention.p: Screenshots auto-delete past a set window, and the whole capture policy is managed centrally by the org.

pricing.title: Free for two years. Then a dollar.
pricing.intro: No trials, no seats to count, no credit card to start. Just install and go.

free.sub: Every feature, every signal, the full web dashboard — free for your first two years.
free.li2: Desktop app, Chrome extension & web dashboard

paid.sub: A single flat dollar a month keeps the hosted service running. That's it.
paid.li3: Or self-host it for free, forever

oss.title: Don't want to pay? Host it yourself.
oss.intro:
BiBoTracking is open source — the Go backend, web dashboard, desktop app (macOS & Windows) and Chrome
           extension are all yours to read, audit and run on your own machine, free forever. The
           hosted service here is just the convenient option.

selfhost.p: One Go binary serves the API and dashboard, backed by Postgres. Your infrastructure, your rules.

inspect.p: Read the source and verify exactly what's captured — and what never leaves the device.

lockin.p: Your data lives in your own SQLite and Postgres. Export it, move it, keep it — anytime.

oss.soon: Star us on GitHub if you find it useful — it really helps.

cta.title: Free for your first two years.
cta.intro: Download the desktop app and sign up — no card, no trial clock. After two years it's just $1/month, or self-host it for free.

personas.title: One tracker, three ways to use it.
personas.intro: Track just yourself, your team, or your family — the same honest signals and local-first privacy. You decide who, if anyone, sees the data.

solo.p: Track your own app time, activity and screenshots — 100% local. No account, and nothing ever leaves your computer.

team.p: Active time, screenshots and activity for staff or freelancers, rolled up for you in one clean web dashboard.

family.p: Keep an eye on your kids' screen time with the same gentle, transparent signals — so you can talk about it together.
